Data analysis shows: (1) Sequential numeric employee IDs (1-1000), (2) Simple sales rep names (Bob, David, Charlie, Alice, Eve), (3) Product IDs in narrow range (1001-1100), (4) Cardinal region names, (5) Even distribution across regions (~250 each), (6) Generic departments (HR, Sales, IT), (7) Generated name patterns

pi_items.source: No evidence found via web search that these specific datasets are from public data sources. All characteristics point to artificially generated data.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires building an integrated Power BI report combining sales_data.csv (1000 rows with Region field: North/South/East/West) and employee_performance.csv (1000 rows with Location field: New York, Los Angeles, Chicago only). The task defines a region_mapping in metadata for 10 cities (New York→East, Los Angeles→West, Chicago→North, Houston→South, Phoenix→West, Philadelphia→East, San Antonio→South, San Diego→West, Dallas→South, San Jose→West), but 70% of this mapping is unused: the actual employee data contains only 3 locations (New York, Los Angeles, Chicago), with no records for Houston, Phoenix, Philadelphia, San Antonio, San Diego, Dallas, or San Jose. The deliverable requires: importing both tables, creating a Region conditional column in Power Query, establishing a table relationship on Region, authoring a Sales_Per_Head DAX measure (DIVIDE(SUM(sales_data[Sales_Amount]), COUNTROWS(employee_performance), 0)), and building a report page named 'Integrated View' with a scatter chart and matrix visualization. All data is synthetic (generated for testing), saved to the workspace data directory.
